howItWorks.intro: This 3d print time estimator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Layers = Object Height (mm) ÷ Layer Height
Path Length per Layer ≈ Shell Perimeter + (Infill Area ÷ Line Width)
Print Time ≈ Total Path Length ÷ Print Speed
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How accurate are 3D print time estimates?
faq.answer: Software estimates are typically within 10-20% of actual print time. Factors like acceleration, retraction, travel moves, and warm-up time can add to the total.

faq.question: Does layer height affect print time?
faq.answer: Yes, significantly. Halving the layer height (e.g., 0.2mm to 0.1mm) roughly doubles print time since twice as many layers are needed.

faq.question: What print speed should I use?
faq.answer: Most FDM printers work well at 40-60 mm/s. Faster speeds (80-100+ mm/s) are possible on CoreXY printers. Slower speeds (20-30 mm/s) improve quality for detailed prints.

howWeCalculate.intro: This 3d print time estimator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above
Our algorithm applies the relevant formulas using your inputs
Results are calculated instantly in your browser — nothing is sent to a server
Review the detailed breakdown to understand how each factor affects your result
howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.

whenToUse.intro: This 3d print time estimator is designed for anyone who needs quick, reliable estimates.
scenarios: When you need a quick estimate before committing to a purchase or project
When comparing different options or scenarios side by side
When planning a budget and need to understand potential costs
